content:
Key Findings

  • Chorley Borough Council paid £149.0M to LANCASHIRE COUNTY COUNCIL between 2021/22 and 2025/26.
  • This single supplier accounted for 40.0% of Chorley's total £372.2M spend.
  • The top 5 suppliers received £239.2M (64.3% of total spend).
  • The top 10 suppliers received £282.9M (76.0% of total spend).
  • £89.3M (24.0%) was paid to suppliers outside the top 10.

Chorley's Significant Investment in Lancashire County Council

Chorley Borough Council's spending data reveals a substantial financial relationship with LANCASHIRE COUNTY COUNCIL, which received £149.0M over five years. This represents nearly 40.0% of Chorley's total expenditure of £372.2M across 21,421 transactions.

This significant allocation suggests a major operational or service delivery partnership between the two councils. The scale of this investment raises questions about the nature of the services provided by LANCASHIRE COUNTY COUNCIL to Chorley and the value this represents for local taxpayers.

Top Suppliers: A Concentrated Spending Pattern

The spending data shows a highly concentrated pattern, with the top 5 suppliers receiving £239.2M (64.3% of total spend). The top 10 suppliers accounted for £282.9M (76.0% of total spend), leaving £89.3M (24.0%) for all other suppliers combined.

This concentration is typical in local government spending, where large contracts for services like waste management, social care, or infrastructure projects dominate budgets. However, the dominance of LANCASHIRE COUNTY COUNCIL as the single largest supplier is notable.

Comparing Chorley's Top Suppliers

Beyond LANCASHIRE COUNTY COUNCIL, Chorley's spending is spread across a mix of government departments and private contractors. The MINISTRY OF HOUSING COMMUNITIES & LOCAL GOVERNMENT received £39.6M (10.6%), while the POLICE & CRIME COMMISSIONER was paid £21.8M (5.8%).

Private contractors like ROBERTSON CE LTD (£11.2M, 3.0%) and JOHN TURNER CONSTRUCTION GROUP LTD (£7.2M, 1.9%) also feature prominently, reflecting Chorley's investment in construction and infrastructure projects.

Rank Supplier Amount % of Total
1 LANCASHIRE COUNTY COUNCIL £149.0M 40.0%
2 MINISTRY OF HOUSING COMMUNITIES & LOCAL GOVERNMENT £39.6M 10.6%
3 POLICE & CRIME COMMISSIONER £21.8M 5.8%
4 RIBBLE VALLEY BOROUGH COUNCIL DIRECT DEBIT ONLY £16.6M 4.5%
5 MINISTRY OF HOUSING,COMMUNITIES&LOCAL GOVERNMENT £12.3M 3.3%

What You Can Do

  • Submit a Freedom of Information request to Chorley Borough Council about the specific services provided by LANCASHIRE COUNTY COUNCIL.
  • Attend council committee meetings, which are open to the public, to learn more about spending decisions.
  • Check the council's published contracts register to see details of agreements with top suppliers.
  • Write to your local councillor to ask about the value for money of the £149.0M spent with LANCASHIRE COUNTY COUNCIL.
  • Visit aidoge.co.uk to explore the full spending data and analyse other transactions.
This article is based on Chorley Borough Council's published spending data covering 2021/22 to 2025/26. All figures are drawn directly from transactions published under the Local Government Transparency Code. Analysis by AI DOGE.
